_comment: Per-chapter ISC subtopics and suggested diagrams. Source of truth for lesson seeding and the `Learn` page subtopic pills. Consumed both by lib/isc-syllabus.ts (app) and scripts/seed-lessons.mjs (local generator).

subtopics: Types of sets (empty, finite, infinite, equal, equivalent)
Subsets, proper subsets, and power sets
Union, intersection, and difference of sets
suggestedDiagrams: Two-set Venn diagram with the union, intersection, and difference regions clearly shaded and labeled
Three-set Venn diagram illustrating inclusion-exclusion for |A∪B∪C|
Number line showing open and closed intervals with correct bracket and parenthesis notation

subtopics: Functions as a special kind of relation
suggestedDiagrams: Arrow diagram from set A to set B distinguishing relations from functions and marking which arrow patterns fail the vertical line test
Two function graphs side by side — one passing the horizontal line test (injective), one failing — to show one-one vs many-one
Mapping diagram showing g ∘ f as a two-stage arrow chain from A → B → C

subtopics: The unit circle and reference angles
Signs of trigonometric ratios in the four quadrants
Graphs and periodicity of sin, cos, tan
suggestedDiagrams: Unit circle with a 30-60-90 angle showing the sin and cos projections labeled on the axes
Graphs of y = sin x and y = cos x on the same axes from 0 to 2π with key points marked
Quadrant chart (ASTC) showing which ratios are positive in each quadrant

subtopics: Statement of the principle of mathematical induction
Proving the inductive step P(k) ⇒ P(k+1)
Common pitfalls (assuming what you must prove)
suggestedDiagrams: Falling-dominoes illustration showing base case (first domino) and inductive step (each pushes the next)
Stacked staircase visual of P(1), P(2), P(3), … with arrows showing the implication P(k) → P(k+1)

subtopics: Definition of i and powers of i
Algebraic form a+bi, addition, subtraction, multiplication
Polar form r(cos θ + i sin θ)
Quadratic equations with real coefficients and complex roots
suggestedDiagrams: Argand plane plotting 3+4i with modulus shown as the line segment to the origin and argument as the angle from the positive real axis
Side-by-side: a parabola y = x² + 1 not crossing the x-axis next to the corresponding complex roots ±i plotted on the Argand plane

subtopics: Half-plane interpretation and the boundary line
Feasible region as an intersection of half-planes
suggestedDiagrams: Number line shaded for the inequality 2x − 3 < 7, with the open/closed endpoint conventions explained
Coordinate plane showing two linear inequalities and their intersection as the shaded feasible region

subtopics: Combinations nCr (selections where order does not matter)
suggestedDiagrams: Tree diagram enumerating the arrangements of 3 letters {A,B,C} to illustrate 3! = 6 permutations
Side-by-side: two boxes labeled 'order matters' (nPr) and 'order doesn't' (nCr), each picking 2 from 4 colored balls

subtopics: Statement of binomial theorem for positive integral index
Coefficient of a specific power of x
Properties of binomial coefficients (sum, alternating sum)
suggestedDiagrams: Pascal's triangle (first 6 rows) with the row n=4 expansion of (a+b)^4 highlighted underneath
Annotated expansion of (x+y)^5 showing where the general term T_(r+1) comes from and how to find the coefficient of x²y³

subtopics: Arithmetic progression: nth term and sum
Geometric progression: nth term and sum
Sum of an infinite geometric series
Recognising AP vs GP from a sequence
suggestedDiagrams: Two number lines stacked: top showing an AP with constant difference d, bottom showing a GP with constant ratio r
Bar chart of the first 6 terms of the GP 2, 4, 8, 16, 32, 64 showing exponential growth

subtopics: Slope of a line and angle of inclination
Distance of a point from a line
suggestedDiagrams: Coordinate plane with one line drawn, its slope triangle marked, and its y-intercept clearly labeled
Two intersecting lines with the acute and obtuse angles between them and the formula tan θ = |(m₁−m₂)/(1+m₁m₂)| annotated

subtopics: Circle: standard equation and general form
Parabola: standard forms y² = 4ax, x² = 4ay; focus, directrix, vertex
Ellipse: standard equation x²/a² + y²/b² = 1; foci, eccentricity, latus rectum
Hyperbola: standard equation x²/a² − y²/b² = 1; foci, asymptotes
Identifying conics from general second-degree equations
suggestedDiagrams: Side-by-side standard sketches of a circle, parabola, ellipse and hyperbola with foci and key parameters labeled
An ellipse with both foci marked and a point P on the curve, showing the defining property PF₁ + PF₂ = 2a

subtopics: Coordinates of a point in space
Coordinates of centroid of a triangle in 3D
suggestedDiagrams: 3D axes with a point P(2,3,4) shown, dropping a perpendicular to the xy-plane, illustrating how the three coordinates locate the point
Line segment AB in 3D with point P dividing it in ratio m:n, illustrating the section formula visually

subtopics: Standard limits: lim (sin x)/x, lim (1 − cos x)/x²
Limits of polynomials and rational functions
Geometrical meaning: derivative as slope of tangent
Differentiation rules: sum, product, quotient, chain (informal)
suggestedDiagrams: Curve y = f(x) with a point approached from both sides on the x-axis, showing the limit value on the y-axis
Tangent line touching a curve at a single point, with the secant lines whose slopes converge to it as Δx → 0

subtopics: Conditional p ⇒ q and biconditional p ⇔ q
Methods of proof: direct, contradiction, contrapositive
suggestedDiagrams: Truth table for p ∧ q, p ∨ q, and ¬p drawn as a clean grid
Flow diagram showing p ⇒ q with its contrapositive ¬q ⇒ ¬p and converse q ⇒ p arranged on a small directed graph

subtopics: Mean deviation about mean and median
Effect of change of origin and scale
suggestedDiagrams: Two dot-plot datasets with the same mean but different spreads, side by side, illustrating variance
Bell-shaped curve with the mean marked and ±1σ, ±2σ regions shaded to illustrate standard deviation

subtopics: Events: simple, compound, mutually exclusive, exhaustive
Addition theorem P(A∪B) = P(A) + P(B) − P(A∩B)
suggestedDiagrams: Sample space tree for two coin tosses with each outcome labeled and its probability
Two overlapping events A and B drawn as a Venn diagram with the addition theorem regions shaded and labeled

subtopics: Types of functions: one-one, onto, bijective
Commutativity, associativity, identity, inverse for binary operations
suggestedDiagrams: Set partition diagram showing equivalence classes from the relation 'a ≡ b (mod 3)' on {0,…,8}
Function f : A → B drawn as an arrow diagram with its inverse f⁻¹ shown reversing every arrow

subtopics: Domain and range of sin⁻¹, cos⁻¹, tan⁻¹
Identities like sin⁻¹ x + cos⁻¹ x = π/2
Sum and difference formulas for inverse trig
Simplification of expressions involving inverse trig
suggestedDiagrams: Graph of y = sin x with the restricted domain [−π/2, π/2] highlighted, alongside the graph of y = sin⁻¹ x obtained by reflecting in y = x
Right triangle with sides labeled so that θ = sin⁻¹(3/5) and cos θ = 4/5 can be read off directly

subtopics: Order of a matrix and types (row, column, square, diagonal, scalar, identity)
Inverse of a matrix by elementary operations
suggestedDiagrams: Visual of a 2×3 matrix multiplied with a 3×2 matrix, with the row-times-column dot product drawn for one entry of the result
A symmetric 3×3 matrix with the diagonal as a mirror line and matching entries A_ij = A_ji highlighted in colour

subtopics: Area of a triangle using determinants
Inverse of a matrix using adjoint
Consistency of a system of linear equations
suggestedDiagrams: 2×2 matrix [[a,b],[c,d]] with the determinant drawn as the signed area of the parallelogram spanned by the column vectors (a,c) and (b,d)
A 3×3 matrix with one cofactor C_23 highlighted: the row and column crossed out and the resulting 2×2 minor shown to the side

subtopics: Continuity at a point and on an interval
Derivatives of exponential and logarithmic functions
Rolle's theorem and Lagrange's mean value theorem
suggestedDiagrams: A function graph showing three failure modes side by side: a removable hole, a jump, and a vertical asymptote — each labeled with the kind of discontinuity
A smooth curve on [a, b] with the chord AB drawn, a tangent line parallel to AB at some c in (a,b), illustrating Lagrange's MVT

subtopics: Tangents and normals to a curve
Maxima and minima — first derivative test
Maxima and minima — second derivative test
Absolute maximum and minimum on a closed interval
suggestedDiagrams: A smooth curve with local maximum, local minimum, and a point of inflection labeled; tangent lines drawn at each showing slope = 0 and the sign-change pattern
An open-top box optimisation: rectangle with corners cut as squares of side x, folded to form a box, with the volume function V(x) plotted alongside showing its maximum

subtopics: Definite integrals as limit of a sum
suggestedDiagrams: Curve y = f(x) on [a, b] with a Riemann sum of vertical rectangles approximating the area under the curve, fading into a smooth shaded region
Two stacked panels: top shows the curve y = f(x); bottom shows F(x) = ∫f(t)dt with the geometric link between the slope of F and the height of f highlighted

subtopics: Area under a curve y = f(x) over [a, b]
Area bounded by a curve and the x-axis
Areas using horizontal strips (integration with respect to y)
Using symmetry to simplify area calculations
suggestedDiagrams: Two curves y = x² and y = x crossing at x = 0 and x = 1, with the area between them shaded and labeled as ∫₀¹ (x − x²) dx
Region bounded by x² + y² = 4 in the first quadrant with the area expressed as ∫₀² √(4−x²) dx and the corresponding angle swept on the circle

subtopics: Order and degree of a differential equation
Formation of a DE from a family of curves
Linear first-order DE and the integrating factor
suggestedDiagrams: Direction field (slope field) for dy/dx = x − y with a couple of solution curves traced through it
Exponential decay curve N(t) = N₀ e^(−kt) with the half-life T marked horizontally and the corresponding value N₀/2 marked vertically

subtopics: Scalar triple product and volume of parallelepiped
suggestedDiagrams: Two vectors a and b in the plane with their sum a + b drawn by the parallelogram law and the projection of a onto b shown as a shadow
Three vectors a, b, c forming a parallelepiped, with the scalar triple product [a b c] highlighted as its signed volume

subtopics: Direction cosines and direction ratios of a line
Vector and Cartesian equation of a line
Shortest distance between two skew lines
Equation of a plane (vector, Cartesian, normal forms)
Distance of a point from a plane
Intersection of a line and a plane
suggestedDiagrams: A line in 3D passing through point A with direction vector d, drawn against the x-, y-, z-axes with direction cosines (l, m, n) labeled as projection angles
Two skew lines in 3D with their common perpendicular drawn, illustrating the shortest distance formula

subtopics: Formulation of a linear programming problem
suggestedDiagrams: Coordinate plane with three constraint lines and the resulting bounded feasible region shaded, corner points labeled with their coordinates
Same feasible region with parallel level lines of the objective function Z = c₁x + c₂y drawn, showing the optimal level line passing through the optimum corner

subtopics: Mean and variance of a random variable
suggestedDiagrams: Two-stage tree diagram for drawing two balls without replacement, with conditional probabilities written on the second-stage branches
Bar chart of the binomial distribution Bin(n=10, p=0.4) with each bar's height equal to P(X=k) and the mean np marked
